title: The British Army
description: The Army takes part in and hosts many public events throughout the year, from musical spectaculars to displays of some of the most up to date equipment in use today by armed forces around the world.
text:
Wednesday 27 August 2025
Infantry Experience 25
Ever wondered what it’s like to be an Infantry soldier in the British Army? Infantry Experience 25 is a one day event for visitors to learn more about careers in the British Army’s Infantry regiments.
Events
3–12 Oct 2025
Brecon, Wales
Exercise Cambrian Patrol 2025
Exercise Cambrian Patrol is the premier patrolling event of the British Army which is held in Wales and hosted by Headquarters 160th (Welsh) Brigade.
30 Mar 2025
Changing of the Guard enhancements in London and Windsor
The public will be able to see more Armed Forces ceremonies, with two parades becoming accessible in London and Windsor.
4 Feb–6 Jul 2025
The Story of Soldier Magazine
Soldier Magazine the official magazine of the British Army, celebrates its 80th birthday this year. Since its creation in March 1945, approved by Field Marshal Bernard Montgomery, its intrepid journalists have reported on every conflict and explored every aspect of service life.
Events
2025
London
Changing of the Guard
Changing of the Guard is a formal ceremony in which the group of soldiers currently protecting Buckingham Palace are replaced by a new group of soldiers.
Events
2025
London
State Visits
State visits are formal visits to the UK by Heads of State from overseas, with the aim of strengthening Britain's relationships with other countries.
Events
9 Nov 2025
Cenotaph, London
Remembrance Sunday
Remembrance Sunday is the Sunday nearest to 11 November - Remembrance Day, which is the anniversary of the end of hostilities in the First World War, in 1918 at 11 am. The national ceremony is held at the Cenotaph on Whitehall, London. Since 2005 the women's memorial has also been included.
